  "content": {
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_01TqAz6JERG55Bh1oBVTXux9",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "     1\u2192#!/usr/bin/env python3\n     2\u2192\"\"\"Generate clean pixel art sprites to replace broken Gemini-generated ones.\"\"\"\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192from PIL import Image, ImageDraw\n     5\u2192import os\n     6\u2192import random\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192ASSETS = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), '..', 'assets')\n     9\u2192random.seed(42)  # Reproducible\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192def save(img, *path_parts):\n    12\u2192    p = os.path.join(ASSETS, *path_parts)\n    13\u2192    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(p), exist_ok=True)\n    14\u2192    img.save(p)\n    15\u2192    print(f\"  \u2713 {'/'.join(path_parts)}\")\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192def fill(size, color):\n    18\u2192    \"\"\"Solid color tile.\"\"\"\n    19\u2192    return Image.new('RGBA', (size, size), color)\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192def noisy_fill(size, base_rgb, variance=10):\n    22\u2192    \"\"\"Solid tile with subtle per-pixel noise for texture.\"\"\"\n    23\u2192    img = Image.new('RGBA', (size, size))\n    24\u2192    for y in range(size):\n    25\u2192        for x in range(size):\n    26\u2192            r = max(0, min(255, base_rgb[0] + random.randint(-variance, variance)))\n    27\u2192            g = max(0, min(255, base_rgb[1] + random.randint(-variance, variance)))\n    28\u2192            b = max(0, min(255, base_rgb[2] + random.randint(-variance, variance)))\n    29\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), (r, g, b, 255))\n    30\u2192    return img\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192# \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n    33\u2192# TILES (16x16, full coverage, no transparency)\n    34\u2192# \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n    35\u2192print(\"=== TILES ===\")\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192# Grass variants\n    38\u2192def make_grass(base, accents, name):\n    39\u2192    img = noisy_fill(16, base, 8)\n    40\u2192    # Add grass blade accents\n    41\u2192    for _ in range(12):\n    42\u2192        x, y = random.randint(0, 15), random.randint(0, 15)\n    43\u2192        c = random.choice(accents)\n    44\u2192        img.putpixel((x, y), (*c, 255))\n    45\u2192    save(img, 'tiles', name)\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192make_grass((74, 124, 63), [(60, 110, 50), (85, 140, 70), (65, 115, 55)], 'grass.png')\n    48\u2192make_grass((90, 140, 79), [(75, 125, 65), (100, 155, 85), (80, 135, 70)], 'grass_alt1.png')\n    49\u2192make_grass((68, 136, 68), [(55, 120, 55), (80, 150, 80), (60, 130, 60)], 'grass_alt2.png')\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192# Path - sandy brown with pebble detail\n    52\u2192img = noisy_fill(16, (194, 168, 107), 8)\n    53\u2192for _ in range(6):\n    54\u2192    x, y = random.randint(1, 14), random.randint(1, 14)\n    55\u2192    img.putpixel((x, y), (175, 150, 90, 255))\n    56\u2192save(img, 'tiles', 'path.png')\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192# Dirt - dark earth\n    59\u2192img = noisy_fill(16, (122, 92, 58), 6)\n    60\u2192for _ in range(5):\n    61\u2192    x, y = random.randint(0, 15), random.randint(0, 15)\n    62\u2192    img.putpixel((x, y), (100, 75, 45, 255))\n    63\u2192save(img, 'tiles', 'dirt.png')\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192# Water - blue with shimmer highlights\n    66\u2192img = noisy_fill(16, (58, 110, 165), 10)\n    67\u2192highlights = [(6,3),(10,7),(3,11),(13,4),(8,13)]\n    68\u2192for hx, hy in highlights:\n    69\u2192    img.putpixel((hx, hy), (100, 160, 220, 255))\n    70\u2192    if hx+1 < 16:\n    71\u2192        img.putpixel((hx+1, hy), (80, 140, 200, 255))\n    72\u2192save(img, 'tiles', 'water.png')\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192# Water edges (water-to-grass transitions)\n    75\u2192for direction, name in [('n','water_edge_n.png'),('s','water_edge_s.png'),('e','water_edge_e.png'),('w','water_edge_w.png')]:\n    76\u2192    img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16))\n    77\u2192    grass = (74, 124, 63, 255)\n    78\u2192    water = (58, 110, 165, 255)\n    79\u2192    for y in range(16):\n    80\u2192        for x in range(16):\n    81\u2192            if direction == 'n':\n    82\u2192                img.putpixel((x,y), grass if y < 6 else (water if y > 8 else (66, 117, 114, 255)))\n    83\u2192            elif direction == 's':\n    84\u2192                img.putpixel((x,y), water if y < 6 else (grass if y > 8 else (66, 117, 114, 255)))\n    85\u2192            elif direction == 'w':\n    86\u2192                img.putpixel((x,y), grass if x < 6 else (water if x > 8 else (66, 117, 114, 255)))\n    87\u2192            elif direction == 'e':\n    88\u2192                img.putpixel((x,y), water if x < 6 else (grass if x > 8 else (66, 117, 114, 255)))\n    89\u2192    save(img, 'tiles', name)\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192# Stone - gray flagstone\n    92\u2192img = noisy_fill(16, (138, 138, 138), 8)\n    93\u2192# Grid lines for flagstone look\n    94\u2192for i in range(16):\n    95\u2192    img.putpixel((i, 7), (115, 115, 115, 255))\n    96\u2192    img.putpixel((i, 8), (115, 115, 115, 255))\n    97\u2192    img.putpixel((7, i), (115, 115, 115, 255))\n    98\u2192    img.putpixel((8, i), (115, 115, 115, 255))\n    99\u2192save(img, 'tiles', 'stone.png')\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192# Wood floor - horizontal planks\n   102\u2192img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16))\n   103\u2192plank_colors = [(139, 109, 74), (149, 119, 84), (130, 100, 65)]\n   104\u2192for y in range(16):\n   105\u2192    base = plank_colors[y // 5 % 3]\n   106\u2192    for x in range(16):\n   107\u2192        if y % 5 == 4:  # Plank gap\n   108\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), (100, 75, 50, 255))\n   109\u2192        else:\n   110\u2192            v = random.randint(-5, 5)\n   111\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), (base[0]+v, base[1]+v, base[2]+v, 255))\n   112\u2192save(img, 'tiles', 'wood_floor.png')\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192# Wall - dark stone bricks\n   115\u2192img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16))\n   116\u2192wall_base = (90, 74, 58)\n   117\u2192for y in range(16):\n   118\u2192    for x in range(16):\n   119\u2192        v = random.randint(-5, 5)\n   120\u2192        # Brick pattern: offset every other row\n   121\u2192        mortar = False\n   122\u2192        if y % 4 == 0:\n   123\u2192            mortar = True\n   124\u2192        elif (y // 4) % 2 == 0 and x % 8 == 0:\n   125\u2192            mortar = True\n   126\u2192        elif (y // 4) % 2 == 1 and (x + 4) % 8 == 0:\n   127\u2192            mortar = True\n   128\u2192        if mortar:\n   129\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), (70, 58, 45, 255))\n   130\u2192        else:\n   131\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), (wall_base[0]+v, wall_base[1]+v, wall_base[2]+v, 255))\n   132\u2192save(img, 'tiles', 'wall.png')\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192# Wall stone variant\n   135\u2192img = noisy_fill(16, (100, 90, 80), 6)\n   136\u2192# Larger stone blocks\n   137\u2192for sy in [0, 8]:\n   138\u2192    for sx in [0, 8]:\n   139\u2192        for y in range(sy, sy+7):\n   140\u2192            img.putpixel((sx+7, y), (75, 65, 55, 255))\n   141\u2192        for x in range(sx, sx+8):\n   142\u2192            img.putpixel((x, sy+7), (75, 65, 55, 255))\n   143\u2192save(img, 'tiles', 'wall_stone.png')\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192# Door - wooden door\n   146\u2192img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16))\n   147\u2192for y in range(16):\n   148\u2192    for x in range(16):\n   149\u2192        if x <= 1 or x >= 14:  # Frame\n   150\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), (80, 60, 40, 255))\n   151\u2192        elif y <= 1:  # Top frame\n   152\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), (80, 60, 40, 255))\n   153\u2192        else:  # Door panels\n   154\u2192            v = random.randint(-3, 3)\n   155\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), (107+v, 74+v, 42+v, 255))\n   156\u2192# Door handle\n   157\u2192img.putpixel((11, 9), (200, 180, 60, 255))\n   158\u2192img.putpixel((11, 10), (200, 180, 60, 255))\n   159\u2192# Center line\n   160\u2192for y in range(2, 16):\n   161\u2192    img.putpixel((7, y), (90, 65, 35, 255))\n   162\u2192    img.putpixel((8, y), (90, 65, 35, 255))\n   163\u2192save(img, 'tiles', 'door.png')\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192# Fence\n   166\u2192img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16), (0,0,0,0))\n   167\u2192fence_c = (107, 91, 58)\n   168\u2192fence_d = (85, 72, 45)\n   169\u2192# Posts at edges\n   170\u2192for y in range(4, 16):\n   171\u2192    img.putpixel((1, y), fence_c)\n   172\u2192    img.putpixel((2, y), fence_c)\n   173\u2192    img.putpixel((13, y), fence_c)\n   174\u2192    img.putpixel((14, y), fence_c)\n   175\u2192# Post tops\n   176\u2192for x in [1,2,13,14]:\n   177\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 3), fence_d)\n   178\u2192# Horizontal rails\n   179\u2192for x in range(16):\n   180\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 7), fence_c)\n   181\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 8), fence_d)\n   182\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 12), fence_c)\n   183\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 13), fence_d)\n   184\u2192save(img, 'tiles', 'fence.png')\n   185\u2192\n   186\u2192# Window - wall with glass\n   187\u2192img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16))\n   188\u2192for y in range(16):\n   189\u2192    for x in range(16):\n   190\u2192        v = random.randint(-3, 3)\n   191\u2192        img.putpixel((x, y), (90+v, 74+v, 58+v, 255))\n   192\u2192# Window pane\n   193\u2192for y in range(3, 13):\n   194\u2192    for x in range(4, 12):\n   195\u2192        if x == 4 or x == 11 or y == 3 or y == 12:  # Frame\n   196\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), (70, 55, 38, 255))\n   197\u2192        elif x == 7 or x == 8 or y == 7 or y == 8:  # Mullion\n   198\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), (70, 55, 38, 255))\n   199\u2192        else:  # Glass\n   200\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), (140, 180, 210, 255))\n   201\u2192# Glass highlight\n   202\u2192img.putpixel((5, 4), (180, 210, 235, 255))\n   203\u2192img.putpixel((6, 5), (170, 200, 225, 255))\n   204\u2192save(img, 'tiles', 'window.png')\n   205\u2192\n   206\u2192# Roof - thatch/tile\n   207\u2192img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16))\n   208\u2192for y in range(16):\n   209\u2192    for x in range(16):\n   210\u2192        # Wave pattern for roof tiles\n   211\u2192        shade = 140 + (10 if (x + y*2) % 4 < 2 else 0) + random.randint(-5, 5)\n   212\u2192        r = min(255, shade + 20)\n   213\u2192        g = min(255, shade - 20)\n   214\u2192        b = min(255, shade - 40)\n   215\u2192        img.putpixel((x, y), (r, g, b, 255))\n   216\u2192save(img, 'tiles', 'roof.png')\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192# Flower - grass with flowers\n   219\u2192img = noisy_fill(16, (74, 124, 63), 8)\n   220\u2192flower_colors = [(220, 80, 80), (220, 180, 60), (180, 100, 200), (255, 140, 100)]\n   221\u2192flower_positions = [(3,3),(8,5),(12,2),(5,10),(10,12),(2,14),(14,9)]\n   222\u2192for i, (fx, fy) in enumerate(flower_positions):\n   223\u2192    c = flower_colors[i % len(flower_colors)]\n   224\u2192    img.putpixel((fx, fy), (*c, 255))\n   225\u2192    # Petals\n   226\u2192    for dx, dy in [(-1,0),(1,0),(0,-1),(0,1)]:\n   227\u2192        nx, ny = fx+dx, fy+dy\n   228\u2192        if 0 <= nx < 16 and 0 <= ny < 16:\n   229\u2192            img.putpixel((nx, ny), (*c, 255))\n   230\u2192save(img, 'tiles', 'flower.png')\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192\n   233\u2192# \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n   234\u2192# OBJECTS (16x16 or 32x32, RGBA transparent bg)\n   235\u2192# \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n   236\u2192print(\"\\n=== OBJECTS ===\")\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192# Bench - wooden park bench, side view\n   239\u2192img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16), (0,0,0,0))\n   240\u2192brown = (139, 100, 60, 255)\n   241\u2192dark = (100, 72, 42, 255)\n   242\u2192# Seat (horizontal planks)\n   243\u2192for x in range(1, 15):\n   244\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 8), brown)\n   245\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 9), brown)\n   246\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 7), dark)\n   247\u2192# Back rest\n   248\u2192for x in range(2, 14):\n   249\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 4), brown)\n   250\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 5), brown)\n   251\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 3), dark)\n   252\u2192# Legs\n   253\u2192for y in range(10, 15):\n   254\u2192    img.putpixel((2, y), dark)\n   255\u2192    img.putpixel((3, y), dark)\n   256\u2192    img.putpixel((12, y), dark)\n   257\u2192    img.putpixel((13, y), dark)\n   258\u2192# Back supports\n   259\u2192for y in range(3, 9):\n   260\u2192    img.putpixel((2, y), dark)\n   261\u2192    img.putpixel((13, y), dark)\n   262\u2192save(img, 'objects', 'bench.png')\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192# Oven (32x32) - stone oven, three states\n   265\u2192def make_oven(name, fire_color=None, smoke=False):\n   266\u2192    img = Image.new('RGBA', (32,32), (0,0,0,0))\n   267\u2192    stone = (130, 120, 110, 255)\n   268\u2192    dark_stone = (100, 90, 80, 255)\n   269\u2192    # Dome shape\n   270\u2192    for y in range(6, 28):\n   271\u2192        for x in range(4, 28):\n   272\u2192            # Rough dome\n   273\u2192            cx, cy = 16, 18\n   274\u2192            dx, dy = x - cx, y - cy\n   275\u2192            if dy > 0 and dy < 12 and abs(dx) < 12:\n   276\u2192                img.putpixel((x, y), stone)\n   277\u2192            elif dy <= 0 and (dx*dx + dy*dy*1.5) < 144:\n   278\u2192                img.putpixel((x, y), stone)\n   279\u2192    # Opening\n   280\u2192    for y in range(18, 26):\n   281\u2192        for x in range(11, 21):\n   282\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), (40, 35, 30, 255))\n   283\u2192    # Chimney\n   284\u2192    for y in range(2, 10):\n   285\u2192        for x in range(18, 22):\n   286\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), dark_stone)\n   287\u2192    # Fire glow\n   288\u2192    if fire_color:\n   289\u2192        for y in range(20, 25):\n   290\u2192            for x in range(13, 19):\n   291\u2192                img.putpixel((x, y), (*fire_color, 255))\n   292\u2192        img.putpixel((15, 19), (*fire_color, 255))\n   293\u2192        img.putpixel((16, 19), (*fire_color, 255))\n   294\u2192    # Smoke particles\n   295\u2192    if smoke:\n   296\u2192        for sx, sy in [(19,1),(20,0),(18,2),(21,1)]:\n   297\u2192            img.putpixel((sx, sy), (180, 180, 180, 150))\n   298\u2192    save(img, 'objects', name)\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192make_oven('oven_off.png')\n   301\u2192make_oven('oven_glow.png', fire_color=(220, 120, 40))\n   302\u2192make_oven('oven_smoke.png', fire_color=(220, 120, 40), smoke=True)\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192# Display case - bakery counter\n   305\u2192def make_display(has_bread):\n   306\u2192    img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16), (0,0,0,0))\n   307\u2192    wood = (139, 109, 74, 255)\n   308\u2192    glass = (180, 210, 230, 180)\n   309\u2192    # Counter base\n   310\u2192    for x in range(1, 15):\n   311\u2192        for y in range(10, 15):\n   312\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), wood)\n   313\u2192    # Glass top\n   314\u2192    for x in range(1, 15):\n   315\u2192        for y in range(4, 10):\n   316\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), glass)\n   317\u2192        img.putpixel((x, 3), (100, 80, 55, 255))\n   318\u2192    # Sides\n   319\u2192    for y in range(3, 15):\n   320\u2192        img.putpixel((0, y), (100, 80, 55, 255))\n   321\u2192        img.putpixel((15, y), (100, 80, 55, 255))\n   322\u2192    if has_bread:\n   323\u2192        # Bread loaves inside\n   324\u2192        for x in range(4, 8):\n   325\u2192            img.putpixel((x, 7), (200, 160, 80, 255))\n   326\u2192            img.putpixel((x, 8), (180, 140, 70, 255))\n   327\u2192        for x in range(9, 13):\n   328\u2192            img.putpixel((x, 7), (200, 160, 80, 255))\n   329\u2192            img.putpixel((x, 8), (180, 140, 70, 255))\n   330\u2192    name = 'display_bread.png' if has_bread else 'display_empty.png'\n   331\u2192    save(img, 'objects', name)\n   332\u2192\n   333\u2192make_display(False)\n   334\u2192make_display(True)\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192# Soil stages\n   337\u2192def make_soil(name, stage):\n   338\u2192    img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16), (0,0,0,0))\n   339\u2192    dirt = (100, 75, 50, 255)\n   340\u2192    # Soil mound\n   341\u2192    for x in range(2, 14):\n   342\u2192        for y in range(10, 15):\n   343\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), dirt)\n   344\u2192    for x in range(3, 13):\n   345\u2192        img.putpixel((x, 9), dirt)\n   346\u2192    for x in range(5, 11):\n   347\u2192        img.putpixel((x, 8), dirt)\n   348\u2192    \n   349\u2192    if stage == 'planted':\n   350\u2192        # Small seed dots\n   351\u2192        img.putpixel((7, 9), (60, 120, 40, 255))\n   352\u2192        img.putpixel((9, 9), (60, 120, 40, 255))\n   353\u2192    elif stage == 'growing':\n   354\u2192        # Small sprouts\n   355\u2192        for x in [6, 9]:\n   356\u2192            img.putpixel((x, 8), (60, 140, 45, 255))\n   357\u2192            img.putpixel((x, 7), (70, 155, 50, 255))\n   358\u2192            img.putpixel((x, 6), (80, 165, 55, 255))\n   359\u2192    elif stage == 'blooming':\n   360\u2192        # Full flowers\n   361\u2192        for x in [5, 8, 11]:\n   362\u2192            stem = (60, 140, 45, 255)\n   363\u2192            img.putpixel((x, 7), stem)\n   364\u2192            img.putpixel((x, 6), stem)\n   365\u2192            img.putpixel((x, 5), stem)\n   366\u2192            # Flower head\n   367\u2192            fc = random.choice([(220, 80, 80, 255), (220, 180, 60, 255), (180, 100, 200, 255)])\n   368\u2192            img.putpixel((x, 4), fc)\n   369\u2192            img.putpixel((x-1, 4), fc)\n   370\u2192            img.putpixel((x+1, 4), fc)\n   371\u2192            img.putpixel((x, 3), fc)\n   372\u2192    elif stage == 'wilted':\n   373\u2192        # Droopy brown stems\n   374\u2192        for x in [6, 9]:\n   375\u2192            img.putpixel((x, 8), (100, 90, 50, 255))\n   376\u2192            img.putpixel((x, 7), (90, 80, 45, 255))\n   377\u2192            img.putpixel((x+1, 6), (80, 70, 40, 255))\n   378\u2192    save(img, 'objects', name)\n   379\u2192\n   380\u2192make_soil('soil_empty.png', 'empty')\n   381\u2192make_soil('soil_planted.png', 'planted')\n   382\u2192make_soil('soil_growing.png', 'growing')\n   383\u2192make_soil('soil_blooming.png', 'blooming')\n   384\u2192make_soil('soil_wilted.png', 'wilted')\n   385\u2192\n   386\u2192# Watering can\n   387\u2192def make_watering_can(full):\n   388\u2192    img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16), (0,0,0,0))\n   389\u2192    metal = (140, 140, 150, 255) if not full else (120, 130, 160, 255)\n   390\u2192    dark = (100, 100, 110, 255)\n   391\u2192    # Body\n   392\u2192    for x in range(3, 11):\n   393\u2192        for y in range(7, 14):\n   394\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), metal)\n   395\u2192    # Top rim\n   396\u2192    for x in range(3, 11):\n   397\u2192        img.putpixel((x, 6), dark)\n   398\u2192    # Spout\n   399\u2192    for i in range(5):\n   400\u2192        img.putpixel((11+i, 9-i), metal)\n   401\u2192        img.putpixel((11+i, 10-i), metal)\n   402\u2192    # Handle\n   403\u2192    for x in range(5, 9):\n   404\u2192        img.putpixel((x, 4), dark)\n   405\u2192    img.putpixel((4, 5), dark)\n   406\u2192    img.putpixel((4, 6), dark)\n   407\u2192    img.putpixel((9, 5), dark)\n   408\u2192    img.putpixel((9, 6), dark)\n   409\u2192    # Water drops if full\n   410\u2192    if full:\n   411\u2192        img.putpixel((14, 6), (100, 160, 220, 255))\n   412\u2192        img.putpixel((15, 5), (100, 160, 220, 255))\n   413\u2192        img.putpixel((13, 5), (100, 160, 220, 255))\n   414\u2192    save(img, 'objects', 'watering_can_full.png' if full else 'watering_can_empty.png')\n   415\u2192\n   416\u2192make_watering_can(False)\n   417\u2192make_watering_can(True)\n   418\u2192\n   419\u2192# Easel states\n   420\u2192def make_easel(name, state):\n   421\u2192    img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16), (0,0,0,0))\n   422\u2192    wood = (120, 90, 55, 255)\n   423\u2192    # Easel legs (A-frame)\n   424\u2192    for y in range(3, 16):\n   425\u2192        img.putpixel((3, y), wood)\n   426\u2192        img.putpixel((12, y), wood)\n   427\u2192    img.putpixel((7, 14), wood)\n   428\u2192    img.putpixel((8, 14), wood)\n   429\u2192    img.putpixel((7, 15), wood)\n   430\u2192    img.putpixel((8, 15), wood)\n   431\u2192    # Canvas area\n   432\u2192    canvas_c = (240, 235, 225, 255)\n   433\u2192    for x in range(4, 12):\n   434\u2192        for y in range(2, 10):\n   435\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), canvas_c)\n   436\u2192    # Canvas border\n   437\u2192    for x in range(4, 12):\n   438\u2192        img.putpixel((x, 1), wood)\n   439\u2192        img.putpixel((x, 10), wood)\n   440\u2192    for y in range(1, 11):\n   441\u2192        img.putpixel((3, y), wood)\n   442\u2192        img.putpixel((12, y), wood)\n   443\u2192    # Shelf\n   444\u2192    for x in range(3, 13):\n   445\u2192        img.putpixel((x, 10), wood)\n   446\u2192    \n   447\u2192    if state == 'sketching':\n   448\u2192        # Pencil lines\n   449\u2192        for i in range(5):\n   450\u2192            img.putpixel((5+i, 4+i//2), (100, 100, 100, 255))\n   451\u2192            img.putpixel((6+i, 6), (100, 100, 100, 255))\n   452\u2192    elif state == 'painting':\n   453\u2192        # Colorful wip\n   454\u2192        colors = [(200,80,80),(80,140,200),(80,180,80),(220,180,60)]\n   455\u2192        for y in range(3, 9):\n   456\u2192            for x in range(5, 11):\n   457\u2192                c = random.choice(colors)\n   458\u2192                img.putpixel((x, y), (*c, 255))\n   459\u2192    elif state == 'finished':\n   460\u2192        # Landscape painting\n   461\u2192        # Sky\n   462\u2192        for x in range(5, 11):\n   463\u2192            for y in range(3, 5):\n   464\u2192                img.putpixel((x, y), (135, 190, 230, 255))\n   465\u2192        # Mountain\n   466\u2192        for i in range(3):\n   467\u2192            img.putpixel((7+i, 4), (120, 140, 120, 255))\n   468\u2192            img.putpixel((8, 3), (120, 140, 120, 255))\n   469\u2192        # Green ground\n   470\u2192        for x in range(5, 11):\n   471\u2192            for y in range(6, 9):\n   472\u2192                img.putpixel((x, y), (80, 150, 70, 255))\n   473\u2192        # Sun\n   474\u2192        img.putpixel((10, 3), (240, 200, 60, 255))\n   475\u2192    save(img, 'objects', name)\n   476\u2192\n   477\u2192make_easel('easel_blank.png', 'blank')\n   478\u2192make_easel('easel_sketching.png', 'sketching')\n   479\u2192make_easel('easel_painting.png', 'painting')\n   480\u2192make_easel('easel_finished.png', 'finished')\n   481\u2192\n   482\u2192# Notice board\n   483\u2192def make_notice_board(has_note):\n   484\u2192    img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16), (0,0,0,0))\n   485\u2192    wood = (120, 90, 55, 255)\n   486\u2192    dark = (90, 65, 38, 255)\n   487\u2192    # Posts\n   488\u2192    for y in range(2, 16):\n   489\u2192        img.putpixel((2, y), dark)\n   490\u2192        img.putpixel((13, y), dark)\n   491\u2192    # Board\n   492\u2192    for x in range(3, 13):\n   493\u2192        for y in range(2, 11):\n   494\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), wood)\n   495\u2192    # Top/bottom frame\n   496\u2192    for x in range(2, 14):\n   497\u2192        img.putpixel((x, 1), dark)\n   498\u2192        img.putpixel((x, 11), dark)\n   499\u2192    if has_note:\n   500\u2192        # Paper note\n   501\u2192        for x in range(5, 11):\n   502\u2192            for y in range(4, 9):\n   503\u2192                img.putpixel((x, y), (240, 235, 210, 255))\n   504\u2192        # Pin\n   505\u2192        img.putpixel((8, 3), (200, 50, 50, 255))\n   506\u2192    save(img, 'objects', name)\n   507\u2192    \n   508\u2192for has_note, name in [(False, 'notice_board.png'), (True, 'notice_board_note.png')]:\n   509\u2192    img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16), (0,0,0,0))\n   510\u2192    wood = (120, 90, 55, 255)\n   511\u2192    dark = (90, 65, 38, 255)\n   512\u2192    for y in range(2, 16):\n   513\u2192        img.putpixel((2, y), dark)\n   514\u2192        img.putpixel((13, y), dark)\n   515\u2192    for x in range(3, 13):\n   516\u2192        for y in range(2, 11):\n   517\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), wood)\n   518\u2192    for x in range(2, 14):\n   519\u2192        img.putpixel((x, 1), dark)\n   520\u2192        img.putpixel((x, 11), dark)\n   521\u2192    if has_note:\n   522\u2192        for x in range(5, 11):\n   523\u2192            for y in range(4, 9):\n   524\u2192                img.putpixel((x, y), (240, 235, 210, 255))\n   525\u2192        img.putpixel((8, 3), (200, 50, 50, 255))\n   526\u2192    save(img, 'objects', name)\n   527\u2192\n   528\u2192\n   529\u2192# \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n   530\u2192# PROPS (if directory exists)\n   531\u2192# \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n   532\u2192print(\"\\n=== PROPS ===\")\n   533\u2192\n   534\u2192# Lantern\n   535\u2192img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16), (0,0,0,0))\n   536\u2192red = (180, 50, 40, 255)\n   537\u2192# Body\n   538\u2192for x in range(5, 11):\n   539\u2192    for y in range(4, 13):\n   540\u2192        img.putpixel((x, y), red)\n   541\u2192# Top/bottom caps\n   542\u2192for x in range(6, 10):\n   543\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 3), (60, 60, 60, 255))\n   544\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 13), (60, 60, 60, 255))\n   545\u2192# Hook\n   546\u2192img.putpixel((7, 2), (60, 60, 60, 255))\n   547\u2192img.putpixel((8, 2), (60, 60, 60, 255))\n   548\u2192img.putpixel((7, 1), (60, 60, 60, 255))\n   549\u2192# Light slit\n   550\u2192for y in range(6, 11):\n   551\u2192    img.putpixel((7, y), (240, 200, 100, 255))\n   552\u2192    img.putpixel((8, y), (240, 200, 100, 255))\n   553\u2192save(img, 'props', 'lantern.png')\n   554\u2192\n   555\u2192# Barrel\n   556\u2192img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16), (0,0,0,0))\n   557\u2192barrel_c = (130, 95, 55, 255)\n   558\u2192band = (80, 80, 90, 255)\n   559\u2192for x in range(3, 13):\n   560\u2192    for y in range(3, 15):\n   561\u2192        img.putpixel((x, y), barrel_c)\n   562\u2192# Metal bands\n   563\u2192for x in range(3, 13):\n   564\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 5), band)\n   565\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 12), band)\n   566\u2192# Rounded top\n   567\u2192for x in range(4, 12):\n   568\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 2), barrel_c)\n   569\u2192save(img, 'props', 'barrel.png')\n   570\u2192\n   571\u2192# Bush\n   572\u2192img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16), (0,0,0,0))\n   573\u2192for y in range(5, 14):\n   574\u2192    for x in range(2, 14):\n   575\u2192        if (x-8)**2 + (y-10)**2 < 25:\n   576\u2192            v = random.randint(-10, 10)\n   577\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), (60+v, 120+v, 50+v, 255))\n   578\u2192# Highlights\n   579\u2192for hx, hy in [(5,7),(9,6),(7,8)]:\n   580\u2192    img.putpixel((hx, hy), (80, 150, 65, 255))\n   581\u2192save(img, 'props', 'bush.png')\n   582\u2192\n   583\u2192# Flower pot\n   584\u2192img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16), (0,0,0,0))\n   585\u2192pot = (180, 100, 60, 255)\n   586\u2192pot_d = (150, 80, 45, 255)\n   587\u2192for x in range(4, 12):\n   588\u2192    for y in range(9, 15):\n   589\u2192        img.putpixel((x, y), pot)\n   590\u2192for x in range(5, 11):\n   591\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 8), pot_d)\n   592\u2192for x in range(3, 13):\n   593\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 8), pot_d)\n   594\u2192# Flower\n   595\u2192stem = (60, 130, 45, 255)\n   596\u2192for y in range(5, 9):\n   597\u2192    img.putpixel((7, y), stem)\n   598\u2192    img.putpixel((8, y), stem)\n   599\u2192fc = (220, 80, 100, 255)\n   600\u2192for dx, dy in [(0,-1),(0,1),(-1,0),(1,0),(0,0)]:\n   601\u2192    img.putpixel((7+dx, 4+dy), fc)\n   602\u2192img.putpixel((7, 3), (240, 220, 60, 255))  # Center\n   603\u2192save(img, 'props', 'flower_pot.png')\n   604\u2192\n   605\u2192# Well\n   606\u2192img = Image.new('RGBA', (16,16), (0,0,0,0))\n   607\u2192stone = (140, 135, 125, 255)\n   608\u2192# Circular well base\n   609\u2192for y in range(8, 15):\n   610\u2192    for x in range(2, 14):\n   611\u2192        if (x-8)**2 + (y-12)**2 < 16:\n   612\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), stone)\n   613\u2192# Water inside\n   614\u2192for y in range(10, 14):\n   615\u2192    for x in range(4, 12):\n   616\u2192        if (x-8)**2 + (y-12)**2 < 10:\n   617\u2192            img.putpixel((x, y), (50, 90, 140, 255))\n   618\u2192# Roof posts\n   619\u2192img.putpixel((3, 4), (100, 75, 50, 255))\n   620\u2192img.putpixel((3, 5), (100, 75, 50, 255))\n   621\u2192img.putpixel((3, 6), (100, 75, 50, 255))\n   622\u2192img.putpixel((3, 7), (100, 75, 50, 255))\n   623\u2192img.putpixel((12, 4), (100, 75, 50, 255))\n   624\u2192img.putpixel((12, 5), (100, 75, 50, 255))\n   625\u2192img.putpixel((12, 6), (100, 75, 50, 255))\n   626\u2192img.putpixel((12, 7), (100, 75, 50, 255))\n   627\u2192# Roof\n   628\u2192for x in range(2, 14):\n   629\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 3), (140, 90, 50, 255))\n   630\u2192    img.putpixel((x, 4), (140, 90, 50, 255))\n   631\u2192save(img, 'props', 'well.png')\n   632\u2192\n   633\u2192print(\"\\n\u2705 All sprites generated!\")\n   634\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. }

Now I understand the codebase structure better. The assets (reading, sit, phone) don't exist yet. I need to: 1. Generate the interaction animation sprites (reading, sit, phone) for all 3 characters (maple, sol, nyx) 2. Add them to the sprite loading in renderer.js (`CHARACTER_SPRITE_PATHS`) 3. Add an `interactAnim` property to the Character class (or a mapping function) 4. Modify `startInteraction()` to track which object type is being interacted with 5. Modify `spriteKey` getter to return the correct sprite during interaction 6. I should check the existing sprite generation scripts to understand how the character sprites are created programmatically so I can follow the same pattern for the new interaction animations.

     1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""Generate café pixel art sprites for AI Aquarium.
     3→Creates detailed 16x16 RGBA PNGs for all café tiles, objects, and props.
     4→Uses PIL drawing primitives with a warm, cozy color palette."""
     5→
     6→import os
     7→from PIL import Image, ImageDraw
     8→
     9→BASE = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
    10→OUT = os.path.join(BASE, "assets", "cafe")
    11→
    12→# Also try Serene Village for outdoor tiles
    13→SV_PATH = os.path.join(BASE, "assets", "research", "limzu", "extracted",
    14→                        "SERENE_VILLAGE_REVAMPED", "Serene_Village_16x16.png")
    15→

    17→os.makedirs(os.path.join(OUT, "objects"), exist_ok=True)

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #45
I see the pattern. The project generates pixel art sprites programmatically using Python/PIL. I need to: 1. Create interaction animation sprites for each character (reading, sit, phone) 2. Add them to the renderer's sprite loading 3. Add a mapping from object IDs to animation names 4. Modify character's `spriteKey` getter to use the interaction animation 5. Track the interaction animation in the `startInteraction()` method Let me first check what's in the existing character sprite generation to understand the character colors better, then create the new sprites. Looking at the existing sprites: - maple: body=#e8a87c, hair=#d4543b, outfit=#f5e6cc (warm barista) - sol: body=#b5835a, hair=#2d5e2a, outfit=#7ab648 (green bookish character) - nyx: body=#c4aed0, hair=#3a2d5e, outfit=#1a1a2e (dark night owl) Now I'll write a Python script to generate the interaction sprites at 48x48 pixels for all three characters, then update the renderer and character files to load and use these new animations.

USERMESSAGE #48
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→#!/usr/bin/env node
     2→// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     3→// Sprite Generator — 16×16 pixel art for AI Aquarium
     4→// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     5→// Usage: node scripts/generate-sprites.js
     6→// Creates assets/tiles/*.png, assets/objects/*.png, assets/characters/*.png
     7→
     8→import { createCanvas } from 'canvas';
     9→import { writeFileSync, mkdirSync } from 'fs';
    10→import { join, dirname } from 'path';
    11→import { fileURLToPath } from 'url';
    12→
    13→const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
    14→const ROOT = join(__dirname, '..');
    15→const SIZE = 16;
    16→
    17→// ── Helpers ──
    18→
    19→function makeCanvas() {
    20→  const c = createCanvas(SIZE, SIZE);
    21→  const ctx = c.getContext('2d');
    22→  ctx.imageSmoothingEnabled = false;
    23→  return { c, ctx };
    24→}
    25→
    26→function save(subdir, name, canvas) {
    27→  const dir = join(ROOT, 'assets', subdir);
    28→  mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });
    29→  const path = join(dir, name + '.png');
    30→  writeFileSync(path, canvas.toBuffer('image/png'));
    31→  console.log(`  ✓ ${subdir}/${name}.png`);
    32→}
    33→
    34→function px(ctx, x, y, color) {
    35→  ctx.fillStyle = color;
    36→  ctx.fillRect(x, y, 1, 1);
    37→}
    38→
    39→function fill(ctx, color) {
    40→  ctx.fillStyle = color;
    41→  ctx.fillRect(0, 0, SIZE, SIZE);
    42→}
    43→
    44→function rect(ctx, x, y, w, h, color) {
    45→  ctx.fillStyle = color;
    46→  ctx.fillRect(x, y, w, h);
    47→}
    48→
    49→// Draw from a 16x16 pixel map (array of strings, each char = color key)
    50→function drawMap(ctx, map, palette) {
    51→  for (let y = 0; y < map.length && y < SIZE; y++) {
    52→    const row = map[y];
    53→    for (let x = 0; x < row.length && x < SIZE; x++) {
    54→      const ch = row[x];
    55→      if (ch === ' ' || ch === '.') continue; // transparent
    56→      const color = palette[ch];
    57→      if (color) px(ctx, x, y, color);
    58→    }
    59→  }
    60→}
    61→
    62→// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    63→// TILE SPRITES
    64→// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    65→
    66→function generateTiles() {
    67→  console.log('
🌿 Generating tile sprites...');
    68→
    69→  // ── grass.png ──
    70→  {
    71→    const { c, ctx } = makeCanvas();
    72→    fill(ctx, '#4a7c3f');
    73→    // Random grass texture
    74→    const highlights = '#5a8c4f';
    75→    const shadows = '#3a6c2f';
    76→    const blades = [
    77→      [2,3],[5,1],[8,6],[11,2],[14,5],[1,9],[4,12],[7,8],
    78→      [10,11],[13,14],[3,7],[6,13],[9,4],[12,9],[15,1],
    79→      [0,14],[7,15],[13,7],[2,11],[8,0]
    80→    ];
    81→    for (const [x, y] of blades) px(ctx, x, y, highlights);
    82→    const darks = [
    83→      [1,4],[4,0],[7,5],[10,3],[13,8],[0,12],[5,10],[8,14],
    84→      [11,7],[14,13],[3,2],[9,9],[12,1],[6,6],[15,11]
    85→    ];
    86→    for (const [x, y] of darks) px(ctx, x, y, shadows);
    87→    save('tiles', 'grass', c);
    88→  }
    89→
    90→  // ── path.png ──
    91→  {
    92→    const { c, ctx } = makeCanvas();
    93→    fill(ctx, '#c2a86b');
    94→    const light = '#d4ba7d';
    95→    const dark = '#a8905a';
    96→    const pebble = '#9a8555';
    97→    // Subtle dirt path texture
    98→    for (const [x, y] of [[2,2],[6,5],[10,3],[14,7],[4,11],[8,14],[12,10],[1,6],[9,1],[15,13]]) {
    99→      px(ctx, x, y, light);
   100→    }

Update characters.js to use the correct sprite during interactions Let me first understand the character colors better by looking at how the characters are defined and what sprites they currently have: Character colors: - maple: body=#e8a87c, hair=#d4543b, outfit=#f5e6cc (warm/pink tones) - sol: body=#b5835a, hair=#2d5e2a, outfit=#7ab648 (green/earth tones) - nyx: body=#c4aed0, hair=#3a2d5e, outfit=#1a1a2e (dark/purple tones) For the interaction sprites, I'm planning three poses: reading with a book held forward and bent posture, sitting cross-legged or in a chair, and a generic phone/interaction pose. USERMESSAGE #55
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
   790→
   791→// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   792→// CHARACTER SPRITES
   793→// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   794→
   795→function generateCharacters() {
   796→  console.log('
🧑 Generating character sprites...');
   797→
   798→  // Character design helper — top-down 16x16 characters
   799→  function drawCharacter(name, colors, variant = 'idle') {
   800→    const { c, ctx } = makeCanvas();
   801→    const { hair, hairDark, skin, shirt, shirtDark, pants, shoes, accessory } = colors;
   802→
   803→    // All characters face downward (top-down perspective, head at top)
   804→    if (variant === 'idle') {
   805→      // Hair/head (rows 2-5)
   806→      rect(ctx, 5, 1, 6, 1, hairDark);
   807→      rect(ctx, 4, 2, 8, 2, hair);
   808→      rect(ctx, 5, 4, 6, 1, hair);
   809→      // Face
   810→      px(ctx, 6, 3, skin); px(ctx, 7, 3, skin); px(ctx, 8, 3, skin); px(ctx, 9, 3, skin);
   811→      px(ctx, 6, 4, skin); px(ctx, 7, 4, skin); px(ctx, 8, 4, skin); px(ctx, 9, 4, skin);
   812→      // Eyes
   813→      px(ctx, 6, 3, '#2a2a2a'); px(ctx, 9, 3, '#2a2a2a');
   814→      // Body/shirt (rows 5-9)
   815→      rect(ctx, 5, 5, 6, 5, shirt);
   816→      px(ctx, 5, 5, shirtDark); px(ctx, 10, 5, shirtDark);
   817→      px(ctx, 5, 9, shirtDark); px(ctx, 10, 9, shirtDark);
   818→      // Arms
   819→      rect(ctx, 3, 6, 2, 3, shirt);
   820→      rect(ctx, 11, 6, 2, 3, shirt);
   821→      px(ctx, 3, 8, skin); px(ctx, 4, 8, skin);
   822→      px(ctx, 11, 8, skin); px(ctx, 12, 8, skin);
   823→      // Pants (rows 10-12)
   824→      rect(ctx, 5, 10, 6, 3, pants);
   825→      px(ctx, 7, 10, pants); px(ctx, 8, 10, pants);
   826→      // Shoes (rows 13-14)
   827→      rect(ctx, 5, 13, 3, 1, shoes);
   828→      rect(ctx, 8, 13, 3, 1, shoes);
   829→      // Accessory
   830→      if (accessory) {
   831→        // Hat/headband
   832→        rect(ctx, 4, 1, 8, 1, accessory);
   833→      }
   834→    } else if (variant === 'walk1') {
   835→      // Walking frame 1 - left foot forward
   836→      // Hair/head
   837→      rect(ctx, 5, 1, 6, 1, hairDark);
   838→      rect(ctx, 4, 2, 8, 2, hair);
   839→      rect(ctx, 5, 4, 6, 1, hair);
   840→      px(ctx, 6, 3, skin); px(ctx, 7, 3, skin); px(ctx, 8, 3, skin); px(ctx, 9, 3, skin);
   841→      px(ctx, 6, 4, skin); px(ctx, 7, 4, skin); px(ctx, 8, 4, skin); px(ctx, 9, 4, skin);
   842→      px(ctx, 6, 3, '#2a2a2a'); px(ctx, 9, 3, '#2a2a2a');
   843→      // Body
   844→      rect(ctx, 5, 5, 6, 5, shirt);
   845→      px(ctx, 5, 5, shirtDark); px(ctx, 10, 5, shirtDark);
   846→      // Arms swinging
   847→      rect(ctx, 3, 5, 2, 3, shirt); // left arm forward
   848→      rect(ctx, 11, 7, 2, 3, shirt); // right arm back
   849→      px(ctx, 3, 7, skin); px(ctx, 4, 7, skin);
   850→      px(ctx, 11, 9, skin); px(ctx, 12, 9, skin);
   851→      // Legs - left forward
   852→      rect(ctx, 4, 10, 3, 3, pants);
   853→      rect(ctx, 9, 10, 3, 2, pants);
   854→      rect(ctx, 4, 13, 3, 1, shoes);
   855→      rect(ctx, 9, 12, 3, 1, shoes);
   856→      if (accessory) rect(ctx, 4, 1, 8, 1, accessory);
   857→    } else if (variant === 'walk2') {
   858→      // Walking frame 2 - right foot forward
   859→      rect(ctx, 5, 1, 6, 1, hairDark);
   860→      rect(ctx, 4, 2, 8, 2, hair);
   861→      rect(ctx, 5, 4, 6, 1, hair);
   862→      px(ctx, 6, 3, skin); px(ctx, 7, 3, skin); px(ctx, 8, 3, skin); px(ctx, 9, 3, skin);
   863→      px(ctx, 6, 4, skin); px(ctx, 7, 4, skin); px(ctx, 8, 4, skin); px(ctx, 9, 4, skin);
   864→      px(ctx, 6, 3, '#2a2a2a'); px(ctx, 9, 3, '#2a2a2a');
   865→      // Body
   866→      rect(ctx, 5, 5, 6, 5, shirt);
   867→      px(ctx, 5, 5, shirtDark); px(ctx, 10, 5, shirtDark);
   868→      // Arms swinging (opposite of walk1)
   869→      rect(ctx, 3, 7, 2, 3, shirt); // left arm back
   870→      rect(ctx, 11, 5, 2, 3, shirt); // right arm forward
   871→      px(ctx, 3, 9, skin); px(ctx, 4, 9, skin);
   872→      px(ctx, 11, 7, skin); px(ctx, 12, 7, skin);
   873→      // Legs - right forward
   874→      rect(ctx, 5, 10, 3, 2, pants);
   875→      rect(ctx, 8, 10, 3, 3, pants);
   876→      rect(ctx, 5, 12, 3, 1, shoes);
   877→      rect(ctx, 8, 13, 3, 1, shoes);
   878→      if (accessory) rect(ctx, 4, 1, 8, 1, accessory);
   879→    }
   880→
   881→    save('characters', `${name}_${variant}`, c);
   882→  }
   883→
   884→  // Maple — baker (warm reds, apron white)
   885→  const mapleColors = {
   886→    hair: '#c06030',
   887→    hairDark: '#a04820',
   888→    skin: '#e0b898',
   889→    shirt: '#e04040',
   890→    shirtDark: '#c03030',
   891→    pants: '#604030',
   892→    shoes: '#4a3020',
   893→    accessory: '#f0f0f0', // white chef hat band
   894→  };
   895→  drawCharacter('maple', mapleColors, 'idle');
   896→  drawCharacter('maple', mapleColors, 'walk1');
   897→  drawCharacter('maple', mapleColors, 'walk2');
   898→
   899→  // Sol — gardener (greens, earth tones)
   900→  const solColors = {
   901→    hair: '#4a3020',
   902→    hairDark: '#3a2010',
   903→    skin: '#d0a878',
   904→    shirt: '#408040',
   905→    shirtDark: '#306030',
   906→    pants: '#6a5a40',
   907→    shoes: '#5a4a30',
   908→    accessory: '#c0a060', // straw hat
   909→  };
   910→  drawCharacter('sol', solColors, 'idle');
   911→  drawCharacter('sol', solColors, 'walk1');
   912→  drawCharacter('sol', solColors, 'walk2');
   913→
   914→  // Nyx — artist (purple, dark tones)
   915→  const nyxColors = {
   916→    hair: '#3a2050',
   917→    hairDark: '#2a1040',
   918→    skin: '#d8b8a0',
   919→    shirt: '#6040a0',
   920→    shirtDark: '#503090',
   921→    pants: '#2a2a3a',
   922→    shoes: '#1a1a2a',
   923→    accessory: '#8060c0', // purple beret
   924→  };
   925→  drawCharacter('nyx', nyxColors, 'idle');
   926→  drawCharacter('nyx', nyxColors, 'walk1');
   927→  drawCharacter('nyx', nyxColors, 'walk2');
   928→}
   929→
   930→// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   931→// RUN
   932→// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   933→
   934→console.log('🎨 AI Aquarium Sprite Generator');
   935→console.log('================================');
   936→generateTiles();
   937→generateObjects();
   938→generateCharacters();
   939→console.log('
✅ All sprites generated!');

const solColors = {\n   901\u2192    hair: '#4a3020',\n   902\u2192    hairDark: '#3a2010',\n   903\u2192    skin: '#d0a878',\n   904\u2192    shirt: '#408040',\n   905\u2192    shirtDark: '#306030',\n   906\u2192    pants: '#6a5a40',\n   907\u2192    shoes: '#5a4a30',\n   908\u2192    accessory: '#c0a060', // straw hat\n   909\u2192  };\n   910\u2192  drawCharacter('sol', solColors, ASSISTANTMESSAGE #56
Now I understand the full picture. The sprites are 16x16 in the generate-sprites.js, but the actual files are 48x48 (3x scale). The script also has walk3 and walk4 references in the character code but only generates walk1 and walk2. Looking at the renderer.js character sprite paths, they have `walk1-walk4` but the generator only makes `walk1-walk2`. This is probably okay since the sprites have been generated somehow. Wait, looking at the directory listing: ``` maple_idle.png maple_walk1.png maple_walk2.png maple_walk3.png maple_walk4.png ``` So walk3 and walk4 exist too. They must have been generated by a newer version. Now I need to generate the new interaction sprites (reading, sit, phone) for all 3 characters at 48x48, update renderer.js with the new sprite paths, and modify characters.js to add an `interactAnim` property that tracks which animation plays during interactions and updates the `spriteKey` getter accordingly. I also need to update `startInteraction()` to accept the animation name and modify engine.js to pass the correct animation when calling it. Looking at the object IDs, I can map them to animations: 'book' and 'desk' objects trigger reading, 'bed' triggers sit, and everything else like register, stove, fridge, and easel defaults to phone interactions. Actually, checking the issue more carefully, there's a specific mapping provided: bookshelf and desk map to reading, bed maps to sit2, and stove, fridge, easel, and other objects map to phone. I'll use sit2 specifically for the bed since that's what the issue specifies. I'm settling on three core animation types: reading for book-related objects, sit for general seating, and phone as a catch-all for interacting with various items. Now I need to establish the file naming pattern—maple_reading.png, maple_sit.png, maple_phone.png, and the same for Sol and Nyx. Before I write the sprite generation script, I'm noticing a discrepancy: the existing generator creates 16x16 sprites, but the actual files in the project are 48x48. I should verify whether these were scaled up after generation or if they came from a different source entirely.

}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #62
Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "python3 -c \"\nfrom PIL import Image\nimg = Image.open('/home/gem/project/assets/characters/maple_idle.png')\npixels = img.load()\n# Print a visual map of non-transparent pixels\nrows = []\nfor y in range(48):\n    row = ''\n    for x in range(48):\n        if pixels[x,y][3] > 0:\n            row += '#'\n        else:\n            row += '.'\n    rows.append(row)\nfor i, r in enumerate(rows):\n    print(f'{i:2}: {r}')\n\" 2>&1"
}

Now I can see the structure of the character sprite at 48x48. The character has a top-down view with: - Rows 0-17: head/upper body area - Rows 18-44: body/legs area - Rows 45-47: feet/shoes Now I need to generate new interaction sprites. The idea is: 1. **reading** - character bends forward, arms extended holding a book 2. **sit** - character in seated position, reduced height, legs spread out 3. **phone** - character stands with arms raised, holding something Let me check if there's a script that generated these 48x48 character sprites:

// Walk animation alternates walk1/walk2 sprites while moving.
// Perception system returns nearby objects and characters within radius.

import { MAP, T, MAP_COLS, MAP_ROWS, TILE_SIZE } from './tilemap.js';

// ── Walkable tiles (everything except walls, fences, water) ──
const UNWALKABLE = new Set([T.WALL, T.WALL_TOP, T.COUNTER, T.COUNTER_L, T.COUNTER_R, T.WINDOW]);

export function isWalkable(tx, ty) {
  if (tx < 0 || tx >= MAP_COLS || ty < 0 || ty >= MAP_ROWS) return false;
  return !UNWALKABLE.has(MAP[ty][tx]);
}

// ── A* Pathfinding ──
// Returns array of {tx, ty} waypoints (excludes start, includes goal),
// or null if no path found. Returns [] if already at goal.

const NEIGHBORS = [[0, -1], [0, 1], [-1, 0], [1, 0]];

export function findPath(sx, sy, gx, gy) {
  if (!isWalkable(gx, gy)) return null;
--
    this.walkFrame = 0;      // 0–3 → walk1/walk2/walk3/walk4
    this.walkTimer = 0;
    this.walkFrameMs = 200;  // ms between frame advances

    // Wander AI
    this.targetObjectId = null;
    this.wanderCooldown = 2000 + Math.random() * 5000; // start soon
    this.interactTimer = 0;

    // Interaction tracking
    this.lastObjectId = null;
    this.pendingAction = 'use';

    // Mood system
    this.mood = 'content';       // happy | content | neutral | upset
    this.moodTimer = 0;          // ms until mood resets to content
  }

  /** Sprite key for the current animation frame. */
  get spriteKey() {
    if (this.action === 'walking') {
      return `${this.id}_walk${this.walkFrame + 1}`;
    }
    return `${this.id}_idle`;
  }

  get moodEmoji() {
    switch (this.mood) {

Returns [] if already at goal.\n\nconst NEIGHBORS = [[0, -1], [0, 1], [-1, 0], [1, 0]];\n\nexport function findPath(sx, sy, gx, gy) {\n  if (!isWalkable(gx, gy)) return null;\n--\n    this.walkFrame = 0;      // 0\u20133 \u2192 walk1/walk2/walk3/walk4\n    this.walkTimer = 0;\n    this.walkFrameMs = 200;  // ms between frame advances\n\n    // Wander AI\n    this.targetObjectId = null;\n    this.wanderCooldown = 2000 + Math.random() * 5000; // start soon\n    this.interactTimer = 0;\n\n    // Interaction tracking\n    this.lastObjectId = null;\n    this.pendingAction = 'use';\n\n    // Mood system\n    this.mood = 'content';       // happy | content | neutral | upset\n    this.moodTimer = 0;          // ms until mood resets to content\n  }\n\n  /** Sprite key for the current animation frame. */\n  get spriteKey() {\n    if (this.action === 'walking') {\n      return `${this.id}_walk${this.walkFrame + 1}`;\n    }\n    return `${this.id}_idle`;\n  }\n\n  get moodEmoji() {\n    switch (this.mood) {",

Now I have a complete picture. I need to: 1. Create a Python script to generate 48x48 interaction sprites (reading, sit, phone) for all 3 characters 2. Update `renderer.js` to add these sprites to `CHARACTER_SPRITE_PATHS` 3. Update `characters.js` to: - Add `interactAnim` property to the Character class - Create a mapping from object ID → animation name - Modify `spriteKey` to use `interactAnim` during interaction - Modify `startInteraction()` to accept animation name 4. Update `engine.js` to determine and pass the animation name when calling `startInteraction()` 5. Now I'm examining an existing character sprite to understand the visual structure and how to draw the interaction poses.

Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "python3 -c \"\nfrom PIL import Image\nfor name in ['maple_idle', 'maple_walk1']:\n    img = Image.open(f'/home/gem/project/assets/characters/{name}.png')\n    pixels = img.load()\n    # Print color map (by hue groups)\n    unique = {}\n    for y in range(48):\n        for x in range(48):\n            p = pixels[x, y]\n            if p[3] > 0:\n                k = p[:3]\n                if k not in unique:\n                    unique[k] = 0\n                unique[k] += 1\n    print(f'--- {name} ---')\n    for col, cnt in sorted(unique.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])[:12]:\n        print(f'  {col}: {cnt}px')\n\" 2>&1"
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #82
OK, the character sprites are detailed 48x48 pixel art with many shades. Rather than trying to exactly replicate the existing style (which would require pixel-by-pixel placement), I'll generate simpler but visually distinct interaction poses that match the character colors. Let me look at the character struct to understand the style better - I'll generate poses that are clearly readable: 1. **reading** - character with book held in front (arms forward, bent) 2. **sit** - character in seated position (cross-legged, top-down view looks like character is compressed) 3. **phone** - character with one arm raised, looking at something Now I'm mapping out the pixel layout of the existing idle sprite to understand where the head, body, and limbs sit, so I can adapt those positions for each pose while keeping the visual style consistent. - Character is seated - body occupies lower half- Head at top- Legs visible sitting to sides (cross-legged) For the **phone** pose: - Head tilted toward raised arm- One arm raised with hand near face (generic "interacting" pose) Looking at this more carefully, I think the best approach is to generate simple but recognizable 48x48 sprites using Python that clearly differentiate the action poses without needing to perfectly match the existing art style. Examining the sprite structure, the character is roughly 24 pixels wide and 48 pixels tall, with the head occupying the top section, torso in the middle, and legs in the lower portion. Since the existing sprites came from LimeZu tilesets and the new variants don't exist yet, I need to create programmatic replacements that maintain the same color palettes and top-down perspective. I'll write a Python script to generate the `reading`, `sit`, and `phone` variants with the appropriate character proportions and warm color schemes.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #93
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/scripts/generate_interaction_sprites.py",
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Generate 48x48 interaction animation sprites for all 3 characters.\n\nGenerates reading, sit, and phone animation sprites used when characters\ninteract with objects. Sprites are drawn in a top-down pixel art style\nmatching the existing idle/walk sprites.\n\nUsage: python3 scripts/generate_interaction_sprites.py\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom PIL import Image, ImageDraw\nimport os\n\nBASE = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))\nOUT = os.path.join(BASE, \"assets\", \"characters\")\nos.makedirs(OUT, exist_ok=True)\n\nSIZE = 48\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Character palettes \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n# Colors matched to existing character sprites (sampled from actual files)\n\nCHARACTERS = {\n    'maple': {\n        # Warm barista: auburn hair, peach skin, cream outfit\n        'skin':       (250, 161, 105),\n        'skin_dark':  (203, 125, 80),\n        'hair':       (157, 85,  60),\n        'hair_dark':  (51,  23,  22),\n        'outfit':     (250, 235, 202),\n        'outfit_dark':(200, 185, 155),\n        'shoes':      (80,  50,  30),\n    },\n    'sol': {\n        # Bookish green: dark hair, warm skin, green outfit\n        'skin':       (195, 145, 100),\n        'skin_dark':  (155, 110,  70),\n        'hair':       (50,  90,  40),\n        'hair_dark':  (30,  60,  20),\n        'outfit':     (100, 165, 65),\n        'outfit_dark':(70,  120, 40),\n        'shoes':      (80,  65,  40),\n    },\n    'nyx': {\n        # Night owl artist: dark purple hair, light skin, dark outfit\n        'skin':       (200, 165, 140),\n        'skin_dark':  (160, 130, 108),\n        'hair':       (58,  45,  95),\n        'hair_dark':  (30,  20,  60),\n        'outfit':     (80,  60, 130),\n        'outfit_dark':(50,  38,  90),\n        'shoes':      (25,  25,  40),\n    },\n}\n\nBOOK_COVER = (80,  50, 140)   # purple book cover\nBOOK_PAGES = (240, 235, 215)  # cream pages\nPHONE_BODY = (50,  50,  55)   # dark phone\n\ndef new_img():\n    return Image.new('RGBA', (SIZE, SIZE), (0, 0, 0, 0))\n\ndef px(img, x, y, color):\n    if 0 <= x < SIZE and 0 <= y < SIZE:\n        img.putpixel((x, y), color + (255,) if len(color) == 3 else color)\n\ndef rect(img, x, y, w, h, color):\n    for dy in range(h):\n        for dx in range(w):\n            px(img, x + dx, y + dy, color)\n\ndef hline(img, x0, x1, y, color):\n    for x in range(x0, x1 + 1):\n        px(img, x, y, color)\n\ndef vline(img, x, y0, y1, color):\n    for y in range(y0, y1 + 1):\n        px(img, x, y, color)\n\n\ndef draw_head(img, cx, top, colors):\n    \"\"\"Draw a top-down head at center cx, starting at row top (height ~12).\"\"\"\n    # Hair top\n    rect(img, cx - 5, top,     10, 3, colors['hair'])\n    rect(img, cx - 4, top + 3,  8, 3, colors['hair'])\n    rect(img, cx - 6, top + 3,  2, 3, colors['hair_dark'])\n    rect(img, cx + 4, top + 3,  2, 3, colors['hair_dark'])\n    # Face row\n    rect(img, cx - 3, top + 6,  6, 5, colors['skin'])\n    # Eyes\n    px(img, cx - 2, top + 7, (40, 30, 25))\n    px(img, cx + 1, top + 7, (40, 30, 25))\n    # Side hair\n    px(img, cx - 4, top + 6, colors['hair'])\n    px(img, cx + 3, top + 6, colors['hair'])\n    px(img, cx - 4, top + 7, colors['hair'])\n    px(img, cx + 3, top + 7, colors['hair'])\n    # Chin/neck\n    rect(img, cx - 2, top + 11, 4, 2, colors['skin'])\n\n\ndef draw_reading(img, colors):\n    \"\"\"Character bent slightly forward, holding an open book in front.\n    Top-down view: head at top, book visible in lower-mid area,\n    arms extended forward.\n    \"\"\"\n    cx = SIZE // 2  # 24\n\n    # Head (rows 2-14)\n    draw_head(img, cx, 2, colors)\n\n    # Upper body / shoulders (rows 14-22)\n    rect(img, cx - 6, 14, 12, 8, colors['outfit'])\n    rect(img, cx - 6, 14,  1, 8, colors['outfit_dark'])\n    rect(img, cx + 5, 14,  1, 8, colors['outfit_dark'])\n\n    # Arms reaching forward / downward (rows 18-32, wide span)\n    rect(img, cx - 11, 20, 5, 8, colors['outfit'])   # left arm\n    rect(img, cx +  6, 20, 5, 8, colors['outfit'])   # right arm\n    # Hands\n    rect(img, cx - 11, 27, 4, 4, colors['skin'])\n    rect(img, cx +  7, 27, 4, 4, colors['skin'])\n\n    # Book held in front (rows 24-38)\n    rect(img, cx - 8, 24, 16, 14, BOOK_COVER)\n    rect(img, cx - 7, 25, 14, 12, BOOK_PAGES)\n    # Book spine (center crease)\n    vline(img, cx, 25, 36, (200, 195, 180))\n    # Text lines on book pages\n    for row_off in [2, 4, 6, 8]:\n        hline(img, cx - 5, cx - 1, 25 + row_off, (180, 175, 165))\n        hline(img, cx + 1, cx + 5, 25 + row_off, (180, 175, 165))\n\n    # Lower body / legs (rows 30-42, partially hidden behind book)\n    rect(img, cx - 4, 38, 8, 8, colors['outfit_dark'])\n    # Feet\n    rect(img, cx - 5, 44, 4, 3, colors['shoes'])\n    rect(img, cx + 1, 44, 4, 3, colors['shoes'])\n\n\ndef draw_sit(img, colors):\n    \"\"\"Character in seated position.\n    Top-down view: head at top, body squished, legs out to sides.\n    \"\"\"\n    cx = SIZE // 2  # 24\n\n    # Head (rows 4-16)\n    draw_head(img, cx, 4, colors)\n\n    # Upper body (rows 16-26)\n    rect(img, cx - 6, 16, 12, 10, colors['outfit'])\n    rect(img, cx - 6, 16,  1, 10, colors['outfit_dark'])\n    rect(img, cx + 5, 16,  1, 10, colors['outfit_dark'])\n\n    # Arms at sides, slightly out\n    rect(img, cx - 11, 17, 5, 7, colors['outfit'])\n    rect(img, cx +  6, 17, 5, 7, colors['outfit'])\n    rect(img, cx - 11, 22, 4, 3, colors['skin'])\n    rect(img, cx +  7, 22, 4, 3, colors['skin'])\n\n    # Seated lower body \u2014 legs folded out\n    # Left leg goes left-down\n    rect(img, cx - 10, 26, 8, 6, colors['outfit_dark'])   # left thigh\n    rect(img, cx - 12, 31, 7, 5, colors['outfit_dark'])   # left shin\n    rect(img, cx - 13, 35, 6, 3, colors['shoes'])         # left foot\n\n    # Right leg goes right-down\n    rect(img, cx +  2, 26, 8, 6, colors['outfit_dark'])   # right thigh\n    rect(img, cx +  5, 31, 7, 5, colors['outfit_dark'])   # right shin\n    rect(img, cx +  7, 35, 6, 3, colors['shoes'])         # right foot\n\n    # Torso lower (row 26-35, center)\n    rect(img, cx - 4, 26, 8, 9, colors['outfit'])\n\n\ndef draw_phone(img, colors):\n    \"\"\"Character looking at / interacting with something (generic action).\n    One arm raised, hand near face holding a small device.\n    Top-down: head at top, right arm raised.\n    \"\"\"\n    cx = SIZE // 2  # 24\n\n    # Head (rows 2-14)\n    draw_head(img, cx, 2, colors)\n\n    # Upper body / shoulders (rows 14-24)\n    rect(img, cx - 6, 14, 12, 10, colors['outfit'])\n    rect(img, cx - 6, 14,  1, 10, colors['outfit_dark'])\n    rect(img, cx + 5, 14,  1, 10, colors['outfit_dark'])\n\n    # Left arm at side (normal)\n    rect(img, cx - 11, 16, 5, 8, colors['outfit'])\n    rect(img, cx - 11, 23, 4, 3, colors['skin'])\n\n    # Right arm raised \u2014 goes up and to the right toward face\n    rect(img, cx + 6, 10, 5, 10, colors['outfit'])   # upper arm (raised)\n    rect(img, cx + 5,  7, 4,  5, colors['skin'])     # hand near head\n    # Phone/device in hand\n    rect(img, cx + 7,  5, 3,  5, PHONE_BODY)         # phone body\n    px(img,  cx + 8,  6, (80, 180, 255))              # screen glow\n\n    # Lower body (rows 24-42)\n    rect(img, cx - 4, 24, 8, 12, colors['outfit_dark'])\n    # Legs\n    rect(img, cx - 5, 36, 4, 8, colors['outfit_dark'])\n    rect(img, cx + 1, 36, 4, 8, colors['outfit_dark'])\n    # Feet\n    rect(img, cx - 6, 43, 4, 3, colors['shoes'])\n    rect(img, cx + 2, 43, 4, 3, colors['shoes'])\n\n\ndef generate_all():\n    poses = {\n        'reading': draw_reading,\n        'sit':     draw_sit,\n        'phone':   draw_phone,\n    }\n\n    for char_name, colors in CHARACTERS.items():\n        for pose_name, draw_fn in poses.items():\n            img = new_img()\n            draw_fn(img, colors)\n            out_path = os.path.join(OUT, f'{char_name}_{pose_name}.png')\n            img.save(out_path)\n            print(f'  \u2713 characters/{char_name}_{pose_name}.png')\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    print('=== Generating interaction sprites ===')\n    generate_all()\n    print('\u2705 Done')\n"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01HMpefcE6zYKmpqMHSDbqQz",
    "name": "Write",
    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/scripts/generate_interaction_sprites.py",
      "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Generate 48x48 interaction animation sprites for all 3 characters.\n\nGenerates reading, sit, and phone animation sprites used when characters\ninteract with objects. Sprites are drawn in a top-down pixel art style\nmatching the existing idle/walk sprites.\n\nUsage: python3 scripts/generate_interaction_sprites.py\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom PIL import Image, ImageDraw\nimport os\n\nBASE = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))\nOUT = os.path.join(BASE, \"assets\", \"characters\")\nos.makedirs(OUT, exist_ok=True)\n\nSIZE = 48\n\n# \u2500\u2500\u2500 Character palettes \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n# Colors matched to existing character sprites (sampled from actual files)\n\nCHARACTERS = {\n    'maple': {\n        # Warm barista: auburn hair, peach skin, cream outfit\n        'skin':       (250, 161, 105),\n        'skin_dark':  (203, 125, 80),\n        'hair':       (157, 85,  60),\n        'hair_dark':  (51,  23,  22),\n        'outfit':     (250, 235, 202),\n        'outfit_dark':(200, 185, 155),\n        'shoes':      (80,  50,  30),\n    },\n    'sol': {\n        # Bookish green: dark hair, warm skin, green outfit\n        'skin':       (195, 145, 100),\n        'skin_dark':  (155, 110,  70),\n        'hair':       (50,  90,  40),\n        'hair_dark':  (30,  60,  20),\n        'outfit':     (100, 165, 65),\n        'outfit_dark':(70,  120, 40),\n        'shoes':      (80,  65,  40),\n    },\n    'nyx': {\n        # Night owl artist: dark purple hair, light skin, dark outfit\n        'skin':       (200, 165, 140),\n        'skin_dark':  (160, 130, 108),\n        'hair':       (58,  45,  95),\n        'hair_dark':  (30,  20,  60),\n        'outfit':     (80,  60, 130),\n        'outfit_dark':(50,  38,  90),\n        'shoes':      (25,  25,  40),\n    },\n}\n\nBOOK_COVER = (80,  50, 140)   # purple book cover\nBOOK_PAGES = (240, 235, 215)  # cream pages\nPHONE_BODY = (50,  50,  55)   # dark phone\n\ndef new_img():\n    return Image.new('RGBA', (SIZE, SIZE), (0, 0, 0, 0))\n\ndef px(img, x, y, color):\n    if 0 <= x < SIZE and 0 <= y < SIZE:\n        img.putpixel((x, y), color + (255,) if len(color) == 3 else color)\n\ndef rect(img, x, y, w, h, color):\n    for dy in range(h):\n        for dx in range(w):\n            px(img, x + dx, y + dy, color)\n\ndef hline(img, x0, x1, y, color):\n    for x in range(x0, x1 + 1):\n        px(img, x, y, color)\n\ndef vline(img, x, y0, y1, color):\n    for y in range(y0, y1 + 1):\n        px(img, x, y, color)\n\n\ndef draw_head(img, cx, top, colors):\n    \"\"\"Draw a top-down head at center cx, starting at row top (height ~12).\"\"\"\n    # Hair top\n    rect(img, cx - 5, top,     10, 3, colors['hair'])\n    rect(img, cx - 4, top + 3,  8, 3, colors['hair'])\n    rect(img, cx - 6, top + 3,  2, 3, colors['hair_dark'])\n    rect(img, cx + 4, top + 3,  2, 3, colors['hair_dark'])\n    # Face row\n    rect(img, cx - 3, top + 6,  6, 5, colors['skin'])\n    # Eyes\n    px(img, cx - 2, top + 7, (40, 30, 25))\n    px(img, cx + 1, top + 7, (40, 30, 25))\n    # Side hair\n    px(img, cx - 4, top + 6, colors['hair'])\n    px(img, cx + 3, top + 6, colors['hair'])\n    px(img, cx - 4, top + 7, colors['hair'])\n    px(img, cx + 3, top + 7, colors['hair'])\n    # Chin/neck\n    rect(img, cx - 2, top + 11, 4, 2, colors['skin'])\n\n\ndef draw_reading(img, colors):\n    \"\"\"Character bent slightly forward, holding an open book in front.\n    Top-down view: head at top, book visible in lower-mid area,\n    arms extended forward.\n    \"\"\"\n    cx = SIZE // 2  # 24\n\n    # Head (rows 2-14)\n    draw_head(img, cx, 2, colors)\n\n    # Upper body / shoulders (rows 14-22)\n    rect(img, cx - 6, 14, 12, 8, colors['outfit'])\n    rect(img, cx - 6, 14,  1, 8, colors['outfit_dark'])\n    rect(img, cx + 5, 14,  1, 8, colors['outfit_dark'])\n\n    # Arms reaching forward / downward (rows 18-32, wide span)\n    rect(img, cx - 11, 20, 5, 8, colors['outfit'])   # left arm\n    rect(img, cx +  6, 20, 5, 8, colors['outfit'])   # right arm\n    # Hands\n    rect(img, cx - 11, 27, 4, 4, colors['skin'])\n    rect(img, cx +  7, 27, 4, 4, colors['skin'])\n\n    # Book held in front (rows 24-38)\n    rect(img, cx - 8, 24, 16, 14, BOOK_COVER)\n    rect(img, cx - 7, 25, 14, 12, BOOK_PAGES)\n    # Book spine (center crease)\n    vline(img, cx, 25, 36, (200, 195, 180))\n    # Text lines on book pages\n    for row_off in [2, 4, 6, 8]:\n        hline(img, cx - 5, cx - 1, 25 + row_off, (180, 175, 165))\n        hline(img, cx + 1, cx + 5, 25 + row_off, (180, 175, 165))\n\n    # Lower body / legs (rows 30-42, partially hidden behind book)\n    rect(img, cx - 4, 38, 8, 8, colors['outfit_dark'])\n    # Feet\n    rect(img, cx - 5, 44, 4, 3, colors['shoes'])\n    rect(img, cx + 1, 44, 4, 3, colors['shoes'])\n\n\ndef draw_sit(img, colors):\n    \"\"\"Character in seated position.\n    Top-down view: head at top, body squished, legs out to sides.\n    \"\"\"\n    cx = SIZE // 2  # 24\n\n    # Head (rows 4-16)\n    draw_head(img, cx, 4, colors)\n\n    # Upper body (rows 16-26)\n    rect(img, cx - 6, 16, 12, 10, colors['outfit'])\n    rect(img, cx - 6, 16,  1, 10, colors['outfit_dark'])\n    rect(img, cx + 5, 16,  1, 10, colors['outfit_dark'])\n\n    # Arms at sides, slightly out\n    rect(img, cx - 11, 17, 5, 7, colors['outfit'])\n    rect(img, cx +  6, 17, 5, 7, colors['outfit'])\n    rect(img, cx - 11, 22, 4, 3, colors['skin'])\n    rect(img, cx +  7, 22, 4, 3, colors['skin'])\n\n    # Seated lower body \u2014 legs folded out\n    # Left leg goes left-down\n    rect(img, cx - 10, 26, 8, 6, colors['outfit_dark'])   # left thigh\n    rect(img, cx - 12, 31, 7, 5, colors['outfit_dark'])   # left shin\n    rect(img, cx - 13, 35, 6, 3, colors['shoes'])         # left foot\n\n    # Right leg goes right-down\n    rect(img, cx +  2, 26, 8, 6, colors['outfit_dark'])   # right thigh\n    rect(img, cx +  5, 31, 7, 5, colors['outfit_dark'])   # right shin\n    rect(img, cx +  7, 35, 6, 3, colors['shoes'])         # right foot\n\n    # Torso lower (row 26-35, center)\n    rect(img, cx - 4, 26, 8, 9, colors['outfit'])\n\n\ndef draw_phone(img, colors):\n    \"\"\"Character looking at / interacting with something (generic action).\n    One arm raised, hand near face holding a small device.\n    Top-down: head at top, right arm raised.\n    \"\"\"\n    cx = SIZE // 2  # 24\n\n    # Head (rows 2-14)\n    draw_head(img, cx, 2, colors)\n\n    # Upper body / shoulders (rows 14-24)\n    rect(img, cx - 6, 14, 12, 10, colors['outfit'])\n    rect(img, cx - 6, 14,  1, 10, colors['outfit_dark'])\n    rect(img, cx + 5, 14,  1, 10, colors['outfit_dark'])\n\n    # Left arm at side (normal)\n    rect(img, cx - 11, 16, 5, 8, colors['outfit'])\n    rect(img, cx - 11, 23, 4, 3, colors['skin'])\n\n    # Right arm raised \u2014 goes up and to the right toward face\n    rect(img, cx + 6, 10, 5, 10, colors['outfit'])   # upper arm (raised)\n    rect(img, cx + 5,  7, 4,  5, colors['skin'])     # hand near head\n    # Phone/device in hand\n    rect(img, cx + 7,  5, 3,  5, PHONE_BODY)         # phone body\n    px(img,  cx + 8,  6, (80, 180, 255))              # screen glow\n\n    # Lower body (rows 24-42)\n    rect(img, cx - 4, 24, 8, 12, colors['outfit_dark'])\n    # Legs\n    rect(img, cx - 5, 36, 4, 8, colors['outfit_dark'])\n    rect(img, cx + 1, 36, 4, 8, colors['outfit_dark'])\n    # Feet\n    rect(img, cx - 6, 43, 4, 3, colors['shoes'])\n    rect(img, cx + 2, 43, 4, 3, colors['shoes'])\n\n\ndef generate_all():\n    poses = {\n        'reading': draw_reading,\n        'sit':     draw_sit,\n        'phone':   draw_phone,\n    }\n\n    for char_name, colors in CHARACTERS.items():\n        for pose_name, draw_fn in poses.items():\n            img = new_img()\n            draw_fn(img, colors)\n            out_path = os.path.join(OUT, f'{char_name}_{pose_name}.png')\n            img.save(out_path)\n            print(f'  \u2713 characters/{char_name}_{pose_name}.png')\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    print('=== Generating interaction sprites ===')\n    generate_all()\n    print('\u2705 Done')\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

Edit TOOL CALL 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/src/characters.js",
  "old_string": "// Walk animation (4-frame cycle: walk1\u2192walk2\u2192walk3\u2192walk4)\n    this.walkFrame = 0;      // 0\u20133 \u2192 walk1/walk2/walk3/walk4\n    this.walkTimer = 0;\n    this.walkFrameMs = 200;  // ms between frame advances\n\n    // Wander AI\n    this.targetObjectId = null;\n    this.wanderCooldown = 2000 + Math.random() * 5000; // start soon\n    this.interactTimer = 0;\n\n    // Interaction tracking\n    this.lastObjectId = null;\n    this.pendingAction = 'use';\n    this.targetCharId = null;   // ID of a character to face toward upon arrival",
  "new_string": "// Walk animation (4-frame cycle: walk1\u2192walk2\u2192walk3\u2192walk4)\n    this.walkFrame = 0;      // 0\u20133 \u2192 walk1/walk2/walk3/walk4\n    this.walkTimer = 0;\n    this.walkFrameMs = 200;  // ms between frame advances\n\n    // Wander AI\n    this.targetObjectId = null;\n    this.wanderCooldown = 2000 + Math.random() * 5000; // start soon\n    this.interactTimer = 0;\n\n    // Interaction tracking\n    this.lastObjectId = null;\n    this.pendingAction = 'use';\n    this.interactAnim = null;    // 'reading' | 'sit' | 'phone' | null (uses idle when null)\n    this.targetCharId = null;   // ID of a character to face toward upon arrival"
}

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/src/characters.js",
  "old_string": "  /** Sprite key for the current animation frame. */\n  get spriteKey() {\n    if (this.action === 'walking') {\n      return `${this.id}_walk${this.walkFrame + 1}`;\n    }\n    return `${this.id}_idle`;\n  }",
  "new_string": "  /** Sprite key for the current animation frame. */\n  get spriteKey() {\n    if (this.action === 'walking') {\n      return `${this.id}_walk${this.walkFrame + 1}`;\n    }\n    if (this.action === 'interacting' && this.interactAnim) {\n      return `${this.id}_${this.interactAnim}`;\n    }\n    return `${this.id}_idle`;\n  }"
}

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/src/characters.js",
  "old_string": "  /** Enter interaction state (after arriving at an object). */\n  startInteraction(objectId) {\n    this.action = 'interacting';\n    this.targetObjectId = objectId;\n    this.lastObjectId = objectId;\n    this.interactTimer = 2000;\n  }",
  "new_string": "  /** Enter interaction state (after arriving at an object).\n   *  @param {string} objectId - ID of the object being interacted with.\n   *  @param {string|null} anim - Animation to play: 'reading' | 'sit' | 'phone' | null.\n   */\n  startInteraction(objectId, anim = null) {\n    this.action = 'interacting';\n    this.targetObjectId = objectId;\n    this.lastObjectId = objectId;\n    this.interactTimer = 2000;\n    this.interactAnim = anim;\n  }"
}

      "new_string": "  /** Enter interaction state (after arriving at an object).\n   *  @param {string} objectId - ID of the object being interacted with.\n   *  @param {string|null} anim - Animation to play: 'reading' | 'sit' | 'phone' | null.\n   */\n  startInteraction(objectId, anim = null) {\n    this.action = 'interacting';\n    this.targetObjectId = objectId;\n    this.lastObjectId = objectId;\n    this.interactTimer = 2000;\n    this.interactAnim = anim;\n  }"

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #123
Edit TOOL CALL 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/src/engine.js",
  "old_string": "  _doInteraction(char, obj, action = 'use') {\n    // Face toward the object\n    const objCx = obj.position.tx + (obj.position.tw || 1) / 2;\n    const objCy = obj.position.ty + (obj.position.th || 1) / 2;\n    const dx = objCx - char.tx;\n    const dy = objCy - char.ty;\n    if (Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)) {\n      char._updateFacing(dx, 0);\n    } else {\n      char._updateFacing(0, dy);\n    }\n\n    const event = this.objectEngine.interact(obj.id, action);\n    if (event) {\n      char.startInteraction(obj.id);\n\n      // Successful interaction \u2192 happy (unless already upset)\n      if (char.mood !== 'upset') {\n        char.setMood('happy', 5000);\n      }\n\n      this._emit('character:interact', {\n        characterId: char.id, characterName: char.name,\n        objectId: obj.id, objectName: event.objectName,\n        from: event.from, to: event.to,\n        produces: event.produces || null,\n        emoji: event.emoji || null,\n      });\n    } else {\n      char.targetObjectId = null;\n    }\n  }",
  "new_string": "  // \u2500\u2500 Object \u2192 interaction animation mapping \u2500\u2500\n  // Maps object ID substrings to the animation played during interaction.\n  static _getInteractAnim(objectId) {\n    if (!objectId) return null;\n    if (objectId.includes('book') || objectId.includes('desk') || objectId.includes('bookshelf')) {\n      return 'reading';\n    }\n    if (objectId.includes('bed') || objectId.includes('kotatsu') || objectId.includes('sofa') || objectId.includes('tv')) {\n      return 'sit';\n    }\n    // phone / general interaction fallback for everything else\n    return 'phone';\n  }\n\n  _doInteraction(char, obj, action = 'use') {\n    // Face toward the object\n    const objCx = obj.position.tx + (obj.position.tw || 1) / 2;\n    const objCy = obj.position.ty + (obj.position.th || 1) / 2;\n    const dx = objCx - char.tx;\n    const dy = objCy - char.ty;\n    if (Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)) {\n      char._updateFacing(dx, 0);\n    } else {\n      char._updateFacing(0, dy);\n    }\n\n    const event = this.objectEngine.interact(obj.id, action);\n    if (event) {\n      const anim = SimulationEngine._getInteractAnim(obj.id);\n      char.startInteraction(obj.id, anim);\n\n      // Successful interaction \u2192 happy (unless already upset)\n      if (char.mood !== 'upset') {\n        char.setMood('happy', 5000);\n      }\n\n      this._emit('character:interact', {\n        characterId: char.id, characterName: char.name,\n        objectId: obj.id, objectName: event.objectName,\n        from: event.from, to: event.to,\n        produces: event.produces || null,\n        emoji: event.emoji || null,\n      });\n    } else {\n      char.targetObjectId = null;\n    }\n  }"
}

  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",

    "input": {
      "replace_all": false,
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/src/engine.js",
      "old_string": "  _doInteraction(char, obj, action = 'use') {\n    // Face toward the object\n    const objCx = obj.position.tx + (obj.position.tw || 1) / 2;\n    const objCy = obj.position.ty + (obj.position.th || 1) / 2;\n    const dx = objCx - char.tx;\n    const dy = objCy - char.ty;\n    if (Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)) {\n      char._updateFacing(dx, 0);\n    } else {\n      char._updateFacing(0, dy);\n    }\n\n    const event = this.objectEngine.interact(obj.id, action);\n    if (event) {\n      char.startInteraction(obj.id);\n\n      // Successful interaction \u2192 happy (unless already upset)\n      if (char.mood !== 'upset') {\n        char.setMood('happy', 5000);\n      }\n\n      this._emit('character:interact', {\n        characterId: char.id, characterName: char.name,\n        objectId: obj.id, objectName: event.objectName,\n        from: event.from, to: event.to,\n        produces: event.produces || null,\n        emoji: event.emoji || null,\n      });\n    } else {\n      char.targetObjectId = null;\n    }\n  }",
      "new_string": "  // \u2500\u2500 Object \u2192 interaction animation mapping \u2500\u2500\n  // Maps object ID substrings to the animation played during interaction.\n  static _getInteractAnim(objectId) {\n    if (!objectId) return null;\n    if (objectId.includes('book') || objectId.includes('desk') || objectId.includes('bookshelf')) {\n      return 'reading';\n    }\n    if (objectId.includes('bed') || objectId.includes('kotatsu') || objectId.includes('sofa') || objectId.includes('tv')) {\n      return 'sit';\n    }\n    // phone / general interaction fallback for everything else\n    return 'phone';\n  }\n\n  _doInteraction(char, obj, action = 'use') {\n    // Face toward the object\n    const objCx = obj.position.tx + (obj.position.tw || 1) / 2;\n    const objCy = obj.position.ty + (obj.position.th || 1) / 2;\n    const dx = objCx - char.tx;\n    const dy = objCy - char.ty;\n    if (Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)) {\n      char._updateFacing(dx, 0);\n    } else {\n      char._updateFacing(0, dy);\n    }\n\n    const event = this.objectEngine.interact(obj.id, action);\n    if (event) {\n      const anim = SimulationEngine._getInteractAnim(obj.id);\n      char.startInteraction(obj.id, anim);\n\n      // Successful interaction \u2192 happy (unless already upset)\n      if (char.mood !== 'upset') {\n        char.setMood('happy', 5000);\n      }\n\n      this._emit('character:interact', {\n        characterId: char.id, characterName: char.name,\n        objectId: obj.id, objectName: event.objectName,\n        from: event.from, to: event.to,\n        produces: event.produces || null,\n        emoji: event.emoji || null,\n      });\n    } else {\n      char.targetObjectId = null;\n    }\n  }"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}
