objects.test.js
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// Object State Machine Tests
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
//
// Minimal test runner — no dependencies needed.
// Run in browser via index.html debug view, or with:
//   node --experimental-vm-modules tests/objects.test.js
//
// Tests use a simple assert/report pattern.

import { ObjectInstance, ObjectEngine } from '../src/objects.js';

let passed = 0;
let failed = 0;
const results = [];

function assert(condition, name) {
  if (condition) {
    passed++;
    results.push({ name, ok: true });
  } else {
    failed++;
    results.push({ name, ok: false });
    console.error(`  FAIL: ${name}`);
  }
}

function test(name, fn) {
  try {
    fn();

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #138
All existing tests pass. Now let me do a quick logic check to verify my implementation makes sense: 1. In `characters.js`: - Added `interactAnim = null` to constructor - Modified `spriteKey` getter to return `${id}_${interactAnim}` when interacting and `interactAnim` is set - Modified `startInteraction(objectId, anim = null)` to accept and store anim - Clear `interactAnim` when interaction ends in `update()` 2. In `engine.js`: - Added static `_getInteractAnim(objectId)` that maps object IDs to animation names: - 'book', 'desk', 'bookshelf' → 'reading' - 'bed', 'kotatsu', 'sofa', 'tv' → 'sit' - everything else → 'phone' - `_doInteraction()` calls `_getInteractAnim(obj.id)` and passes to `startInteraction()` 3. In `renderer.js`: - Added paths for `*_reading`, `*_sit`, `*_phone` sprites for all 3 characters The logic covers all the interaction cases—like `cafe_book` containing 'book' maps to the 'reading' animation, and the sprite key construction ensures the right character variant loads for each interaction type.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #147
Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "node --input-type=module << 'EOF'\n// Quick logic test: verify getInteractAnim mapping\nclass SimulationEngine {\n  static _getInteractAnim(objectId) {\n    if (!objectId) return null;\n    if (objectId.includes('book') || objectId.includes('desk') || objectId.includes('bookshelf')) {\n      return 'reading';\n    }\n    if (objectId.includes('bed') || objectId.includes('kotatsu') || objectId.includes('sofa') || objectId.includes('tv')) {\n      return 'sit';\n    }\n    return 'phone';\n  }\n}\n\nconst tests = [\n  ['cafe_book', 'reading'],\n  ['apt_desk', 'reading'],\n  ['apt_bookshelf', 'reading'],\n  ['apt_bed', 'sit'],\n  ['kotatsu_1', 'sit'],\n  ['living_tv', 'sit'],\n  ['apt_stove', 'phone'],\n  ['apt_fridge', 'phone'],\n  ['apt_easel', 'phone'],\n  ['cafe_espresso', 'phone'],\n  ['cafe_register', 'phone'],\n  ['cafe_sink', 'phone'],\n  [null, null],\n];\n\nlet allOk = true;\nfor (const [id, expected] of tests) {\n  const got = SimulationEngine._getInteractAnim(id);\n  const ok = got === expected;\n  console.log(`${ok ? '\u2705' : '\u274c'} ${id} \u2192 ${got} (expected ${expected})`);\n  if (!ok) allOk = false;\n}\nconsole.log(allOk ? '\\n\u2705 All mapping tests pass' : '\\n\u274c Some tests failed');\nEOF"
}

      "command": "node --input-type=module << 'EOF'\n// Quick logic test: verify getInteractAnim mapping\nclass SimulationEngine {\n  static _getInteractAnim(objectId) {\n    if (!objectId) return null;\n    if (objectId.includes('book') || objectId.includes('desk') || objectId.includes('bookshelf')) {\n      return 'reading';\n    }\n    if (objectId.includes('bed') || objectId.includes('kotatsu') || objectId.includes('sofa') || objectId.includes('tv')) {\n      return 'sit';\n    }\n    return 'phone';\n  }\n}\n\nconst tests = [\n  ['cafe_book', 'reading'],\n  ['apt_desk', 'reading'],\n  ['apt_bookshelf', 'reading'],\n  ['apt_bed', 'sit'],\n  ['kotatsu_1', 'sit'],\n  ['living_tv', 'sit'],\n  ['apt_stove', 'phone'],\n  ['apt_fridge', 'phone'],\n  ['apt_easel', 'phone'],\n  ['cafe_espresso', 'phone'],\n  ['cafe_register', 'phone'],\n  ['cafe_sink', 'phone'],\n  [null, null],\n];\n\nlet allOk = true;\nfor (const [id, expected] of tests) {\n  const got = SimulationEngine._getInteractAnim(id);\n  const ok = got === expected;\n  console.log(`${ok ? '\u2705' : '\u274c'} ${id} \u2192 ${got} (expected ${expected})`);\n  if (!ok) allOk = false;\n}\nconsole.log(allOk ? '\\n\u2705 All mapping tests pass' : '\\n\u274c Some tests failed');\nEOF"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #158
Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nFRG-243: play contextual animations during object interactions\n\nCharacters now play visually appropriate animations when interacting with\nobjects instead of remaining in the idle pose throughout the interaction.\n\n- Add reading, sit, phone sprite assets (48x48) for all 3 characters\n- Add scripts/generate_interaction_sprites.py to reproduce the sprites\n- Add interactAnim field to Character; spriteKey returns correct sprite\n  during interactions; cleared automatically on interaction end\n- Add SimulationEngine._getInteractAnim() mapping: book/desk/bookshelf \u2192\n  reading, bed/kotatsu/sofa/tv \u2192 sit, everything else \u2192 phone\n- Pass the resolved animation to startInteraction() in _doInteraction()\n- Load new sprite paths in renderer.js CHARACTER_SPRITE_PATHS\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\""
}

      "command": "git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nFRG-243: play contextual animations during object interactions\n\nCharacters now play visually appropriate animations when interacting with\nobjects instead of remaining in the idle pose throughout the interaction.\n\n- Add reading, sit, phone sprite assets (48x48) for all 3 characters\n- Add scripts/generate_interaction_sprites.py to reproduce the sprites\n- Add interactAnim field to Character; spriteKey returns correct sprite\n  during interactions; cleared automatically on interaction end\n- Add SimulationEngine._getInteractAnim() mapping: book/desk/bookshelf \u2192\n  reading, bed/kotatsu/sofa/tv \u2192 sit, everything else \u2192 phone\n- Pass the resolved animation to startInteraction() in _doInteraction()\n- Load new sprite paths in renderer.js CHARACTER_SPRITE_PATHS\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\""
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #162
Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "gh pr create \\\n  --title \"FRG-243: Characters play contextual animations during interactions\" \\\n  --body \"$(cat <<'EOF'\n## Summary\n- Characters now display contextual animation sprites during object interactions instead of freezing in the idle pose\n- Added `reading`, `sit`, and `phone` animation sprites (48\u00d748) for all 3 characters (maple, sol, nyx)\n- Implemented an object-ID-to-animation mapping: book/desk \u2192 `reading`, bed/tv \u2192 `sit`, all others \u2192 `phone` fallback\n\n## What changed\n- `assets/characters/`: 9 new sprite files (`*_reading.png`, `*_sit.png`, `*_phone.png`)\n- `scripts/generate_interaction_sprites.py`: reproducible sprite generation script\n- `src/characters.js`: `interactAnim` field on `Character`; `spriteKey` getter returns the contextual sprite during interactions; cleared on interaction end\n- `src/engine.js`: `SimulationEngine._getInteractAnim(objectId)` maps object IDs to animation names; passed into `startInteraction()`\n- `src/renderer.js`: new sprite paths added to `CHARACTER_SPRITE_PATHS`\n\n## Test plan\n- [ ] Launch the sim and observe Sol interacting with `cafe_book` \u2014 should show `reading` sprite\n- [ ] Observe any character interacting with `apt_desk` \u2014 should show `reading` sprite\n- [ ] Observe any character interacting with `apt_bed` \u2014 should show `sit` sprite\n- [ ] Observe characters interacting with stove/fridge/easel/espresso \u2014 should show `phone` sprite\n- [ ] Confirm character returns to `idle` sprite after interaction timer expires\n- [ ] Existing 44 object state-machine tests still pass (`node --experimental-vm-modules tests/objects.test.js`)\n\nCloses FRG-243\n\n\ud83e\udd16 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nEOF\n)\""
}

      "command": "gh pr create \\\n  --title \"FRG-243: Characters play contextual animations during interactions\" \\\n  --body \"$(cat <<'EOF'\n## Summary\n- Characters now display contextual animation sprites during object interactions instead of freezing in the idle pose\n- Added `reading`, `sit`, and `phone` animation sprites (48\u00d748) for all 3 characters (maple, sol, nyx)\n- Implemented an object-ID-to-animation mapping: book/desk \u2192 `reading`, bed/tv \u2192 `sit`, all others \u2192 `phone` fallback\n\n## What changed\n- `assets/characters/`: 9 new sprite files (`*_reading.png`, `*_sit.png`, `*_phone.png`)\n- `scripts/generate_interaction_sprites.py`: reproducible sprite generation script\n- `src/characters.js`: `interactAnim` field on `Character`; `spriteKey` getter returns the contextual sprite during interactions; cleared on interaction end\n- `src/engine.js`: `SimulationEngine._getInteractAnim(objectId)` maps object IDs to animation names; passed into `startInteraction()`\n- `src/renderer.js`: new sprite paths added to `CHARACTER_SPRITE_PATHS`\n\n## Test plan\n- [ ] Launch the sim and observe Sol interacting with `cafe_book` \u2014 should show `reading` sprite\n- [ ] Observe any character interacting with `apt_desk` \u2014 should show `reading` sprite\n- [ ] Observe any character interacting with `apt_bed` \u2014 should show `sit` sprite\n- [ ] Observe characters interacting with stove/fridge/easel/espresso \u2014 should show `phone` sprite\n- [ ] Confirm character returns to `idle` sprite after interaction timer expires\n- [ ] Existing 44 object state-machine tests still pass (`node --experimental-vm-modules tests/objects.test.js`)\n\nCloses FRG-243\n\n\ud83e\udd16 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nEOF\n)\""
    },

      "type": "direct"

USERMESSAGE #165
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
{"id":"ddf62c48-94a0-40cd-9a7a-cdc23aabc2af","identifier":"FRG-243","title":"Characters play contextual animations during interactions","description":"## Problem

Characters play idle_anim during all interactions. Sol reads a bookshelf but stands frozen. Maple uses the stove but looks like she is just standing there.

## Available Assets (48x48 LimeZu sprites)

All 3 characters (Amelia/Lucy/Ash) have:

* reading_48x48.png — for bookshelf, desk
* sit_48x48.png / sit2 / sit3 — for kotatsu, TV, bed
* phone_48x48.png — general interaction fallback

## What to do

1. Map each object to an interaction animation: bookshelf->reading, desk->reading, kotatsu->sit, tv->sit, bed->sit2, stove->phone, fridge->phone, tea_set->phone, easel->phone, bonsai->phone
2. Load these spritesheets in scene.js
3. In startInteract(), play the mapped animation instead of idle_anim
4. On interaction end, return to idle_anim

## Acceptance Criteria

* Each object interaction plays a visually appropriate animation
* Animation plays for the full interaction duration
* Character returns to idle animation when interaction ends","projectMilestone":{"id":"a6eb53b7-72b4-453e-9e23-aa42a629b7a4","name":"M2: Visual sim (main page)"},"priority":{"value":1,"name":"Urgent"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-243/characters-play-contextual-animations-during-interactions","gitBranchName":"devlab/frg-243-characters-play-contextual-animations-during-interactions","createdAt":"2026-03-09T04:29:22.729Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-09T08:41:58.994Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Test in Progress","labels":[],"attachments":[{"id":"7704eea7-749c-4530-a926-07c965cdbb12","title":"FRG-243: Characters play contextual animations during interactions","subtitle":null,"url":"https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium/pull/4"}],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Project Manager","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","assignee":"Project Manager","assigneeId":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"AI Aquarium","projectId":"0385a8c7-ba5a-43a3-b223-acf4d1ad6d63","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #168
## Status DONE ## What Changed - `assets/characters/maple_reading.png` / `sol_reading.png` / `nyx_reading.png` — reading pose sprites - `assets/characters/maple_sit.png` / `sol_sit.png` / `nyx_sit.png` — sitting pose sprites - `assets/characters/maple_phone.png` / `sol_phone.png` / `nyx_phone.png` — phone/general-interact pose sprites - `scripts/generate_interaction_sprites.py` — Python script to regenerate all 9 sprites - `src/characters.js` — added `interactAnim` field; `spriteKey` returns contextual sprite during interactions; `startInteraction(objectId, anim)` accepts animation name; cleared on interaction end - `src/engine.js` — added `SimulationEngine._getInteractAnim(objectId)` mapping and passes result to `startInteraction()` - `src/renderer.js` — added 9 new sprite paths to `CHARACTER_SPRITE_PATHS` ## Key Decisions - The issue referenced LimeZu asset filenames (`reading_48x48.png` etc.) that didn't exist in the repo, so generated programmatic 48×48 sprites that match each character's colour palette - Used a single `phone` animation as the generic fallback (covers stove, fridge, easel, espresso, register, sink, etc.) rather than creating separate sprites for every object - Mapping is string-substring-based on object IDs (`includes('book')` → reading, `includes('bed')` → sit, else → phone), keeping it self-documenting and trivially extensible ## PR / Branch - Branch: `feature/frg-243-contextual-interaction-animations` - PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium/pull/4
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "text",
    "text": "## Status\nDONE\n\n## What Changed\n- `assets/characters/maple_reading.png` / `sol_reading.png` / `nyx_reading.png` \u2014 reading pose sprites\n- `assets/characters/maple_sit.png` / `sol_sit.png` / `nyx_sit.png` \u2014 sitting pose sprites\n- `assets/characters/maple_phone.png` / `sol_phone.png` / `nyx_phone.png` \u2014 phone/general-interact pose sprites\n- `scripts/generate_interaction_sprites.py` \u2014 Python script to regenerate all 9 sprites\n- `src/characters.js` \u2014 added `interactAnim` field; `spriteKey` returns contextual sprite during interactions; `startInteraction(objectId, anim)` accepts animation name; cleared on interaction end\n- `src/engine.js` \u2014 added `SimulationEngine._getInteractAnim(objectId)` mapping and passes result to `startInteraction()`\n- `src/renderer.js` \u2014 added 9 new sprite paths to `CHARACTER_SPRITE_PATHS`\n\n## Key Decisions\n- The issue referenced LimeZu asset filenames (`reading_48x48.png` etc.) that didn't exist in the repo, so generated programmatic 48\u00d748 sprites that match each character's colour palette\n- Used a single `phone` animation as the generic fallback (covers stove, fridge, easel, espresso, register, sink, etc.) rather than creating separate sprites for every object\n- Mapping is string-substring-based on object IDs (`includes('book')` \u2192 reading, `includes('bed')` \u2192 sit, else \u2192 phone), keeping it self-documenting and trivially extensible\n\n## PR / Branch\n- Branch: `feature/frg-243-contextual-interaction-animations`\n- PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium/pull/4"
  }
}
